name: =?utf-8?Q?Supervisor=20David=20Canepa?=
subject: 2023 January e-newsletter
body:
| | | |
---
| | |
# 2023 January e-newsletter
News and information for District 5 in San Mateo County.
---
|
---
|
---
|
#### **Board of Supervisors welcomes new members**
---
| Dear friends,

I’m delighted to offer congratulations to both Noelia Corzo and Ray Mueller on their swearing in as new members of the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors. I am especially proud of Corzo for making history as the first Latina to hold this position in the county’s history. We know that with her leadership, San Mateo County will continue to thrive and be a place where all members of the community can succeed.

I would also like to extend congratulations to Dave Pine, who was appointed President of the Board of Supervisors, and Warren Slocum, the Vice President of the Board.
---
Supervisor Pine said his goal for 2023 will be to “facilitate a highly effective and productive culture among the Board of Supervisors that will enable us to make the best decisions possible for the good of the county.” I couldn’t agree more.

While we have two new members, the Board’s goals remain to reach functional zero homelessness, create more affordable housing opportunities, foster economic development, protect public health, prepare for climate change and potential disasters and care for the most vulnerable in our community with a focus on equity and social justice.

I’m excited to get to work with my new colleagues to improve the lives of all of the county’s residents.
---
| **County declares state of emergency, braces for more storms** The recent heavy rains with more on the way the next 7 to 10 days prompted San Mateo County to declare a local state of emergency leading up to Wednesday’s major storm that knocked out power for thousands of mostly coastside and north county residents. As you can see in the picture above, county also activated its Emergency Operations Center to serve as the around-the-clock nerve center that coordinates resources and communication with local, regional and state agencies and response partners.

After a series of intense storms, now is the time to prepare for power outages, road closures and other troubles.

The county has also activated its 2-1-1 line that people can call if they need emergency assistance including shelter. We have secured about 160 beds on the Bayshore and the coast at local hotels if necessary and will activate the College of San Mateo as a mass evacuation site if necessary. I urge all to stay home and stay off the roads during the upcoming intense storms. Stay safe and make sure your family and elders are prepared for power outages with flashlights, batteries and non-perishable food.
---
---
| **New County Office Building 3 will be one of the greenest in the U.S., slated for completion at end 2023** If you’ve visited downtown Redwood City recently, who have seen the progress we have made on construction of our new County Office Building #3 which will be the new home of the Board of Supervisors Chambers and offices and other county departments within the year hopefully.

With the county’s ambitious sustainability goals, when complete this will be perhaps the greenest civic buildings in the nation. Since San Mateo County is particularly vulnerable to the perils of climate change, including wildfire, drought, and sea-level rise our goal is to create a climate-friendly government center that will serve county residents for decades to come.
With its structural design that uses cross-laminated timber to reduce embodied carbon, COB3 will stand as a testament to San Mateo County’s environmental stewardship.

Once completed, the building will have 5 stories of office and meeting space that will accommodate 500 County employees. The building is designed Skidmore, Owings and Merrill and the construction manager being Truebeck with plans for the project to be completed by the end of 2023.

COB3 is one of many projects the county is constructing in the most ambitious $700 million 5-year capital improvement plan the county has undertaken including the construction of the Cordilleras Mental Health Facility in unincorporated Redwood City, new Health Campus in San Mateo, the South San Francisco Campus project at the old traffic court site and Navigation Center to house the homeless, among other projects.

These new buildings are built with the environment in mind and the well-being of county workers and the tens of thousands of individuals they serve every year.
---
| **I’m proud to join the SamTrans Board of Directors** I was proud to be sworn-in to the SamTrans Board of Directors on Wednesday, Jan. 4 and am ready to work with my fellow board members to improve public transit for the people who need it the most, older persons and individuals with disabilities and of course our students who rely on it to get to school.

As you may already know, my constituents in Daly City and north San Mateo County comprise the largest chunk of ridership and without SamTrans would be unable to get to work, the grocery store or medical appointments.

My mission is to make this public transportation agency the most efficient and greenest in the Bay Area, with an eye on increasing ridership, especially along the El Camino corridor near job centers and the Caltrain line.

Speaking of Caltrain, as a board member on the Metropolitan Transportation Commission, I was proud to vote in March on the approval of a Regional Grant Strategy that added three critical Peninsula projects to our regional list including the electrification of Caltrain and grade separations in Burlingame, South San Francisco and San Bruno. The plan maximizes Federal investment in our critical regional projects from the Biden Administration’s Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.

We now have a clear path to fully fund the long-delayed electrification of Caltrain project which will speed up the removal of its diesel fleet that creates so much pollution and by prioritizing the grade separation in Burlingame at Broadway, this MTC commitment will do away with California’s most dangerous railroad crossing.

The SamTrans board consists of nine members: three members who are appointed by the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors, two of whom are members of the Board of Supervisors and one who possesses expertise in the field of transportation; three members appointed by the City Selection Committee of the Council of Cities, who are council members representing the northern, central and southern portions of the county; and three public members, one of whom must be a coastside resident. Board members terms are four years.

For additional information about board members, visit:

[https://www.samtrans.com/about-samtrans/board-directors](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=cddcad7270&e=c0babbc643).

In addition to serving on SamTrans and the MTC, my other committee assignments include serving on the: California Identification System (Cal ID); California State Association of Counties (CSAC) Board of Directors; Community Corrections Partnership (CCP); San Mateo Health Commission (HPSM); Juvenile Justice & Delinquency Prevention Commission (JJDPC); Peninsula Traffic Congestion Relief Alliance; San Mateo Medical Center (SMMC) Board of Directors; Urban Counties of California (UCC) Board of Directors; Vehicle Theft Task Force; COVID 19/Public Health Emergency Response; and the San Mateo County Fire Resiliency Steering Committee.

As you can see I have a busy schedule ahead for 2023 and look forward to continue to communicate with you all on what our office is accomplishing.
---
| **[](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=0c6ed90f42&e=c0babbc643)
Share your ideas for new recreational facilities at San Bruno Mountain** San Bruno Mountain State and County Park is a gem in north San Mateo County that offers vast open space in the middle of some dense urban areas. San Mateo County Parks is currently exploring expanding visitor facilities in San Bruno Mountain State & County Park, in the Saddle field area just north of the main parking lot.

Our Parks Department has also been assessing what types of activities residents would like to see at the park which include bike rides, nature hikes and wildlife viewing?

San Bruno Mountain Park is an open space island amongst itself surrounded by dense urban areas. It is underutilized and we need your help to find ways to get more families and children into the park.

Copy and paste [https://www.smcgov.org/parks/SBM-Survey](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=f4a7c06853&e=c0babbc643) into your web browser to take the survey.

**Battling food insecurity **
Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: **CalFresh:**
Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: [https://www.cdss.ca.gov/food-nutrition/calfresh](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=faa2d85e06&e=c0babbc643) Contact: (650) 802-6576, [antorres@smcgov.org](mailto:antorres@smcgov.org) **Daly City Partnership:**
DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: [https://www.dcpartnership.org/covid19/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=da37f701d3&e=c0babbc643) **Second Harvest Food Bank:**
Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: [https://www.shfb.org/get-food/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=57c039bcbb&e=c0babbc643) Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 **Interns wanted to join our team **

We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9. You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org) In friendship, David J. Canepa
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | |
---
| _Copyright © 2023 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Supervisor David Canepa1618 Sullivan497Daly City, CA 94015
[Add us to your address book](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/vcard?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96&e=c0babbc643&c=566888cbb9) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96&e=c0babbc643&c=566888cbb9).

[](http://www.mailchimp.com/email-referral/?utm_source=freemium_newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=referral_marketing&aid=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&afl=1)
---

These new buildings are built with the environment in mind and the well-being of county workers and the tens of thousands of individuals they serve every year. | I’m proud to join the SamTrans Board of Directors I was proud to be sworn-in to the SamTrans Board of Directors on Wednesday, Jan. 4 and am ready to work with my fellow board members to improve public transit for the people who need it the most, older persons and individuals with disabilities and of course our students who rely on it to get to school. As you may already know, my constituents in Daly City and north San Mateo County comprise the largest chunk of ridership and without SamTrans would be unable to get to work, the grocery store or medical appointments. My mission is to make this public transportation agency the most efficient and greenest in the Bay Area, with an eye on increasing ridership, especially along the El Camino corridor near job centers and the Caltrain line. Speaking of Caltrain, as a board member on the Metropolitan Transportation Commission, I was proud to vote in March on the approval of a Regional Grant Strategy that added three critical Peninsula projects to our regional list including the electrification of Caltrain and grade separations in Burlingame, South San Francisco and San Bruno. The plan maximizes Federal investment in our critical regional projects from the Biden Administration’s Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. We now have a clear path to fully fund the long-delayed electrification of Caltrain project which will speed up the removal of its diesel fleet that creates so much pollution and by prioritizing the grade separation in Burlingame at Broadway, this MTC commitment will do away with California’s most dangerous railroad crossing. The SamTrans board consists of nine members: three members who are appointed by the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors, two of whom are members of the Board of Supervisors and one who possesses expertise in the field of transportation; three members appointed by the City Selection Committee of the Council of Cities, who are council members representing the northern, central and southern portions of the county; and three public members, one of whom must be a coastside resident. Board members terms are four years. For additional information about board members, visit: . In addition to serving on SamTrans and the MTC, my other committee assignments include serving on the: California Identification System (Cal ID); California State Association of Counties (CSAC) Board of Directors; Community Corrections Partnership (CCP); San Mateo Health Commission (HPSM); Juvenile Justice & Delinquency Prevention Commission (JJDPC); Peninsula Traffic Congestion Relief Alliance; San Mateo Medical Center (SMMC) Board of Directors; Urban Counties of California (UCC) Board of Directors; Vehicle Theft Task Force; COVID 19/Public Health Emergency Response; and the San Mateo County Fire Resiliency Steering Committee. As you can see I have a busy schedule ahead for 2023 and look forward to continue to communicate with you all on what our office is accomplishing. | Share your ideas for new recreational facilities at San Bruno Mountain San Bruno Mountain State and County Park is a gem in north San Mateo County that offers vast open space in the middle of some dense urban areas. San Mateo County Parks is currently exploring expanding visitor facilities in San Bruno Mountain State & County Park, in the Saddle field area just north of the main parking lot. Our Parks Department has also been assessing what types of activities residents would like to see at the park which include bike rides, nature hikes and wildlife viewing? San Bruno Mountain Park is an open space island amongst itself surrounded by dense urban areas. It is underutilized and we need your help to find ways to get more families and children into the park. Copy and paste into your web browser to take the survey. Battling food insecurity Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: CalFresh: Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: Contact: (650) 802-6576, mailto:antorres@smcgov.org Daly City Partnership: DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: Second Harvest Food Bank: Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 Interns wanted to join our team We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: . You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org In friendship, David J. Canepa | | | | | | | Copyright © 2023 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Congressman=20Paul=20Gosar?=
subject: Gosar Votes to Make Historic Changes In Congress
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GOSAR FOR CONGRESS
body:
An Unprecedented Win for We the People | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/drpaulgosar/gosar-votes-to-make-historic-changes-in-congress?e=645b3b1338)
---
| | | [ ](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=437a66f755&e=645b3b1338)
---
| | | | | |
## Gosar Votes to Make Historic Changes In Congress
“An Unprecedented Win for We the People”
---

Dear Peter, | |
##
---

Congressman Paul A. Gosar, D.D.S. (AZ-09) issued the following statement regarding his vote in favor of Representative Kevin McCarthy as Speaker of the United States House of Representatives:

**Congress begins with an unprecedented win for “We the People.” **

**[We have won the most open and transparent rules ever seen in the United States Congress. We have won a better budget process. And we have won a return to the constitutional process. These victories will change the course of our nation](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=2bdd632426&e=645b3b1338). **

When this week started those of us opposed to the status quo were told we couldn't make a difference, but we have, and we will continue to.

_**[](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=716f35be32&e=645b3b1338)**_

**[I was elected in the Tea Party wave, and I have represented the people of Arizona by keeping my promises and that is just what you have seen this week. What the American people saw this week was exactly what voters ask me for time and time again: a government for the people, by the people, done with transparency and accountability, not behind closed doors in smoke-filled rooms](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=3938faba96&e=645b3b1338). **

President Donald Trump has called me a "Warrior in Congress" for the America First Movement, and all warriors must know when, and most importantly who to fight. We have won the fight within the Republican party, and most importantly the fight for the ideals our Founding Fathers built this nation on.

After 11 votes against, I have cast my 12th vote for Kevin McCarthy, voting with Representatives Jim Jordan, Byron Donalds, Chip Roy, and with support from President Trump – because we all know that now is the time to fight the Biden administration and the new woke socialist Left.

**[The people wanted me to drain the swamp. The swamp is not a person. It’s the rules. The rules that govern the people. I changed the rules. I changed the swamp](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=70d12571db&e=645b3b1338). **

_**[](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=e87d91f0ce&e=645b3b1338)**_

**What Others Are Saying:**

**American for Limited Government, Rick Manning: **

_“The House rules agreed to by Kevin McCarthy will empower conservatives in the House to fight for limited government principles and fiscal sanity. The agreement to put a ‘Church-like Committee’ in the Judiciary Committee under Jim Jordan will help unravel the abuse of power by federal government intelligence agencies giving hope to the American people that these rabid dogs will be caged and shackled for a generation. America stands at a precipice where our spending threatens our economic future and the wholesale attacks on the First, Fourth and Fifth Amendments by the FBI and others threaten our personal liberties.

“Our nation owes a tremendous debt of gratitude to Representatives Chip Roy, Paul Gosar, Scott Perry, Byron Donalds, Josh Brecheen, Michael Cloud, Andrew Clyde, Anna Paulina Luna, Mary Miller, Ralph Norman, Andy Ogles, Dan Bishop, Andy Harris and Keith Self for fighting to end autocratic rule in the House of Representatives.”_

**Representative Scott Perry: **

_“We’re at a turning point. I’ve negotiated in good faith, with one purpose: to restore the People’s House back to its rightful owners. The framework for an agreement is in place, so in a good-faith effort, I voted to restore the People’s House by voting for Kevin McCarthy.”_

**Representative Michael Cloud:**

_“I came to Washington with the understanding that Congress was broken. The reforms we negotiated ...will be a massive change in how the House operates and enables members of Congress to truly represent their constituents.”_

**Former Representative Lee Zeldin:**

_“The status quo of the House of Representatives needed to be changed, not just challenged. The reforms the House GOP will now be implementing will make Congress and our nation stronger. The law few days have been a messy process, but the light at the end of the tunnel is beautiful.”_
---

---

**[The people wanted me to drain the swamp. The swamp is not a person. It’s the rules. The rules that govern the people. I changed the rules. I changed the swamp](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=3f116510ad&e=645b3b1338).

Help us keep fighting by making a contribution to mobilize patriots in Congress!**
[**_CONTRIBUTE $250_**](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=3d78514b87&e=645b3b1338)

[**_CONTRIBUTE $100_**](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=d726b7f8cd&e=645b3b1338)

[**_CONTRIBUTE $50 _**](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=90c283c432&e=645b3b1338)

[**_CONTRIBUTE ANY AMOUNT_**](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=0569afc8fe&e=645b3b1338)

_**[](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=a67db1448e&e=645b3b1338)**_

Respectfully,
[](https://drpaulgosar.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7c5af6d9acae2e4f6ee86cbd&id=bdb7e1b44f&e=645b3b1338)
Paul Gosar, D.D.S.

clean_body: An Unprecedented Win for We the People | | | | | | | | | | | | | Gosar Votes to Make Historic Changes In Congress “An Unprecedented Win for We the People” Dear Peter, | | Congressman Paul A. Gosar, D.D.S. (AZ-09) issued the following statement regarding his vote in favor of Representative Kevin McCarthy as Speaker of the United States House of Representatives: Congress begins with an unprecedented win for “We the People.” . When this week started those of us opposed to the status quo were told we couldn't make a difference, but we have, and we will continue to. . President Donald Trump has called me a "Warrior in Congress" for the America First Movement, and all warriors must know when, and most importantly who to fight. We have won the fight within the Republican party, and most importantly the fight for the ideals our Founding Fathers built this nation on. After 11 votes against, I have cast my 12th vote for Kevin McCarthy, voting with Representatives Jim Jordan, Byron Donalds, Chip Roy, and with support from President Trump – because we all know that now is the time to fight the Biden administration and the new woke socialist Left. . What Others Are Saying: American for Limited Government, Rick Manning: “The House rules agreed to by Kevin McCarthy will empower conservatives in the House to fight for limited government principles and fiscal sanity. The agreement to put a ‘Church-like Committee’ in the Judiciary Committee under Jim Jordan will help unravel the abuse of power by federal government intelligence agencies giving hope to the American people that these rabid dogs will be caged and shackled for a generation. America stands at a precipice where our spending threatens our economic future and the wholesale attacks on the First, Fourth and Fifth Amendments by the FBI and others threaten our personal liberties. “Our nation owes a tremendous debt of gratitude to Representatives Chip Roy, Paul Gosar, Scott Perry, Byron Donalds, Josh Brecheen, Michael Cloud, Andrew Clyde, Anna Paulina Luna, Mary Miller, Ralph Norman, Andy Ogles, Dan Bishop, Andy Harris and Keith Self for fighting to end autocratic rule in the House of Representatives.” Representative Scott Perry: “We’re at a turning point. I’ve negotiated in good faith, with one purpose: to restore the People’s House back to its rightful owners. The framework for an agreement is in place, so in a good-faith effort, I voted to restore the People’s House by voting for Kevin McCarthy.” Representative Michael Cloud: “I came to Washington with the understanding that Congress was broken. The reforms we negotiated ...will be a massive change in how the House operates and enables members of Congress to truly represent their constituents.” Former Representative Lee Zeldin: “The status quo of the House of Representatives needed to be changed, not just challenged. The reforms the House GOP will now be implementing will make Congress and our nation stronger. The law few days have been a messy process, but the light at the end of the tunnel is beautiful.” . Help us keep fighting by making a contribution to mobilize patriots in Congress! Respectfully, Paul Gosar, D.D.S. | | PAID FOR BY GOSAR FOR CONGRESS | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2023 Paul Gosar for Congress, All rights reserved.
